whatThisIs: Check the eight-item Pulmonary Embolism Rule-out Criteria (Kline 2004): age <50, HR <100, SpO2 >=95% on room air, no hemoptysis, no exogenous estrogen, no prior DVT/PE, no recent surgery or trauma, and no unilateral leg swelling. If every criterion is satisfied in a patient who is already low pretest probability, PE can be ruled out without further workup.
whenToUse: Apply PERC only after clinical gestalt or Wells has already established a low pretest probability for PE. PERC is a rule-out aid for that specific population - it is not a screening test, and a single failed criterion does not diagnose PE; it only means PERC cannot be used and the workup continues with D-dimer or imaging.
